title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
hints: Consider a dynamic programming approach where dp[i] represents the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at index i.
To compute dp[i] efficiently, you can maintain an auxiliary array 'tails' where tails[k] stores the smallest tail of all increasing subsequences of length k+1.
Use binary search to find the position to update or extend the 'tails' array for each element in nums, ensuring strictly increasing order.
